diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Rate-controlled AF with anxiety-driven sympathetic response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Consistent with an accelerated ventricular response and/or anxiety; non-specific on its own.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Rapid atrial fibrillation with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Perceived palpitation does not quantify the true rate; needs measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete lapse of daily beta-blocker (bisoprolol) supply — missed/upcoming dose

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Loss of rate control / rebound tachycardia
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt beta-blocker withdrawal can precipitate rebound sympathetic activity and an accelerated AF ventricular response; drives the same-day bridging need.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Rapid atrial fibrillation with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patient-counted rate is unreliable and cannot confirm a true tachycardia or its haemodynamic significance; it flags the need for measurement, not a diagnosis.
Clinician-measured rate/rhythm — sealed; the AI Doctor cannot obtain this and must infer the possibility from history, then route for measurement.
Clinician-measured, no home cuff reported — sealed; not obtainable by the AI Doctor in this encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregularly irregular rhythm on auscultation, no acute heart-failure signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear lung bases and no peripheral oedema on examination argue against acute failure — but this requires the clinician's hands/stethoscope and is sealed.
Absence of ischaemic-type pain lowers but does not exclude ACS; ECG/troponin (unavailable via chat) would be needed to exclude.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Rapid atrial fibrillation with haemodynamic compromise
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No syncope reduces the likelihood of significant haemodynamic compromise at present.

symptoms.symptom_name: No severe rest dyspnoea or leg swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No rest breathlessness or oedema by history argues against acute failure, pending examination.
Afebrile with no source effectively excludes an infective driver of the tachycardia.
Acute anxiety raises heart rate and blood pressure and generates palpitation sensation; a contributing, not sole, explanation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent electrolytes and renal function normal (digital health record)

investigation_details.expected_result: Within normal limits (verified on the digital health record)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Uncertainty about safety of continued beta-blocker/rate-control prescribing; relevant to the in-person prescriber, not obtainable by the AI Doctor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal renal function and electrolytes support ongoing prescribing — a derived reference fact for the prescriber, sealed from the AI Doctor.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person measured heart rate and blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step the AI cannot do remotely — quantify the ventricular rate and BP to distinguish benign anxiety-driven tachycardia from rapid AF with compromise; obtain same-day.
Characterise the rhythm and exclude ischaemia in an older patient with palpitations and a fast irregular pulse.
Confirms safety of continued rate-control prescribing; already recent/normal on the record, so a review item rather than an urgent re-order.
